title: Prototype temporal-context defer experiment
notes: Implement a prototype defer follow-up with temporal outer-step context features from per-step scores and probe drift; evaluate held-out against prototype_hybrid and close if it does not beat the live lead.

title: Switch-gated prototype ensemble experiment
description: Add a per-state switch-gated prototype ensemble head that learns when to use the sharp shared-projection branch versus the broader agreement-mixture branch, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid and prototype_agree_mix_hybrid, and close it unless it improves the sparse-positive frontier without broad regression.

title: Run combined multiheavy plus path-reranker contender batch
description: Combine the new lead exploit curriculum (multiheavy) with the path reranker, run a matched contender batch on the rebalanced suites, and test whether the gains are additive before revisiting calibration heads or new architectural branches.
close_reason: Completed 3-seed combined multiheavy plus reranker contender; mean regret 1.23, p95 4.69, miss 39.6%, slightly better than plain multiheavy.

title: Round3 X6 ablations and exploit robustness tuning
description: Run targeted X6 ablations plus exploit-side regret/deadline robustness tuning with balanced GPU usage.

title: Stabilize combined reranker under deeper/heavy OOD
description: Investigate traffic-aware gating, fallback-to-multiheavy logic, or explicit OOD regularization so the multiheavy plus reranker recipe stops exploding on deeper-packets and heavy-dynamic rebalanced OOD suites.
close_reason: Traffic-gated reranker stabilization completed and not promoted: base-suite behavior matched multiheavy on seeds 311/312/313, seed311 OOD improved, but deeper_packets6 seed312 remained catastrophically unstable.

title: Test path-first selection mode on multiheavy
Round6 repo audit and branch setup
merge-to-main
description: Merge completed round8 work back to main locally and attempt final push.

title: Evidence-calibrated agreement mixture experiment
description: Add an evidence-calibrated agreement-mixture prototype head that lets the shared/dual blend gate see internal positive and negative prototype evidence from both branches, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid and prototype_agree_mix_hybrid, and close it unless it improves the sparse-positive frontier without broad regression.

title: Stress-test combined multiheavy plus reranker on rebalanced OOD suites
description: Run the new lead exploit recipe on the rebalanced branching/deeper-packets/heavy-dynamic OOD suites and compare against plain multiheavy on regret, p95 regret, deadline miss rate, and verifier-confirmed feasibility.
close_reason: Completed 3-seed OOD stress batch: combined reranker improved branching OOD but catastrophically destabilized deeper-packets OOD on seeds 311/312, so plain multiheavy remains the robust exploit default.

title: Agreement-plus-soft-tail blend experiment
description: Test whether blending the live support-weighted agreement-mixture score with the new soft-tail score can keep the sub-1% frontier gain while recovering the stronger matched-band aggregate quality of the original branch.

title: Expand verifier-filter reranker to matched multi-seed confirmation
description: Take the seed312 verifier-backed path-feasibility filter rescue and run the minimal confirmation batch: matched seed311/313 training plus paired rebalanced OOD sweeps, then decide whether verifier-filter reranking is robust enough to promote over plain multiheavy for path-level stress cases.
close_reason: Completed the matched verifier-filter reranker confirmation across seeds 311/312/313 plus paired OOD sweeps. The branch improved 3-seed OOD mean regret from 6.45 to 3.15 and miss rate from 95.8% to 50.0%, but matched in-distribution mean regressed because seed313 worsened, so it stays an OOD-specialized branch rather than the new default.

title: Prototype evidence defer experiment
description: Add a prototype-evidence defer head that uses top positive and negative prototype similarities as the decision readout, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid, and close it unless it improves stable-positive recovery or the hard near-tie band without broad regression.
notes: Testing whether the sparse positive family depends on the fine pattern of prototype evidence rather than a single pooled prototype score. Variants: plain evidence head and evidence+risk hybrid.

title: Test packets6 train-only multiheavy curriculum
description: Increase the training-split hidden-corridor packet count cap from 4 to 6 for plain multiheavy while keeping validation and test manifests fixed at the current shared suites. Goal: see whether a materially harder multi-packet training distribution improves held-out regret and deadline behavior.
close_reason: Completed matched 3-seed packets6-train scout. The training split packet cap changed from 4 to 6 with fixed val/test manifests, but the held-out rollout converged back to the plain multiheavy baseline on all three seeds.

title: Family-split prototype defer experiment
notes: Implement a triage prototype defer head with explicit positive/neutral/harmful memory banks, evaluate on the round12 held-out split, and compare against prototype_hybrid and round11 margin_regime.

title: Branch-calibrated sharp negative-tail support agreement mixture experiment
description: Test whether the live support-weighted agreement-mixture branch improves if shared and dual negative banks use separate learned sharpness-gate centers and slopes instead of one global gate schedule. Success means preserving or improving the sub-1% 75%/90.73% lane or recovering more recall around 1% without giving back aggregate regret.

title: Risk-conditioned support agreement mixture experiment
description: Test whether the live support-weighted agreement-mixture branch improves if per-prototype support weights are allowed to shift per state from the cheap risk features, instead of staying static across the entire bank.

title: round9 frontier pack and guard
description: Build reusable frontier pack, machine-readable manifests, and automatic guard reporting for hard near-tie promotion.

title: Support-weighted agreement mixture experiment
description: Add bounded per-prototype support weighting inside the shared and dual banks of the live agreement-mixture prototype family, evaluate whether bank-level weighting can improve matched-band coverage efficiency or aggregate quality without broad regressions, and update reports.

title: Prototype lift defer experiment
description: Add a prototype defer head that preserves the live prototype score and only allows a nonnegative lift residual on top, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid, and close it unless it improves stable-positive recovery or the hard near-tie band without broad regression.
notes: Testing whether the live prototype lead is bottlenecked by residual branches that can suppress useful positives. Variants: positive-only lift residual and gated positive-only lift residual.

title: Round7 poly-constructor scout
close_reason: completed: poly-constructor scout killed early after catastrophic epoch-1 rollout collapse

title: Round2 exploit track: E3 stress and ablations
description: Reproduce the current best exploit run, then run A1/A2 continuation experiments with balanced GPU usage and update reports.

title: distillation conditional
description: Distill search-improved near-tie decisions back into a deployable constructor only if search helps.
close_reason: round8 completed or explicitly scoped out

title: Support-weighted memory agreement blend experiment
description: Add per-prototype support logits to the live memory-agreement blend so the head can suppress diffuse bank members inside the memory/shared/dual prototype banks, evaluate whether support-weighted retrieval improves micro-budget precision or matched-band recall without reopening broad false positives, and update reports.

title: Prototype multiscale defer experiment
description: Add a multiscale prototype defer head that combines local prototype-bank evidence with a coarse positive/negative centroid branch, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid, and close it unless it improves stable-positive recovery or the hard near-tie band without broad regression.
notes: Testing whether sparse stable-positive transfer needs both local prototype retrieval and a coarse global family anchor. Variants: plain multiscale and multiscale+higher-risk hybrid.

title: Round7 contingent promotion branch
close_reason: scoped-out: no round7 constructor branch earned promotion to contingent follow-up

title: Revisit auxiliary deadline heads only with rollout-aware checkpoint selection on plain multiheavy
description: Auxiliary deadline/slack heads remain calibration-positive but rollout-flat. Only revisit on plain multiheavy if checkpoint selection or policy coupling changes so held-out rollout can move.
close_reason: Fixed trainer best-checkpoint evaluation, reran the plain multiheavy deadline-head scout, and confirmed the selected checkpoint still matched multiheavy at 1.50 regret, 5.96 p95, and 43.8% deadline miss; branch stays negative.

title: Prototype pair-context hybrid experiment
notes: Implement a prototype pair-context hybrid defer experiment using decision-feature prototypes plus a lightweight top-2 pair summary branch; evaluate held-out against prototype_hybrid and close if it does not beat the current lead.

title: Branchwise negative-cleanup lift support agreement mixture experiment
description: Test whether the successful branchwise-max fusion should instead be implemented as separate positive-only fixed-cleanup lifts inside the shared and dual branches before the outer agreement mixture, to preserve the sharp low-coverage lane while borrowing higher-budget recall.

title: Test calibration or verifier add-ons on top of combined exploit leader
description: Revisit the deadline/slack head or verifier-backed final-step shaping only if it improves the combined multiheavy plus reranker recipe's tail behavior without erasing its current rollout gains.
close_reason: Combined deadline/slack head scout finished flat: auxiliary calibration improved, but seed311 held-out test rollout exactly matched the combined baseline and was not promoted.

title: Test integrated candidate-path head on multiheavy
Dual-sharp negative-tail support agreement experiment
description: Test whether the live negative-tail and sharp-negative-tail gains can be combined by applying sharpness-gated negative-bank cleanup only on the dual branch while keeping fixed negative-tail cleanup on the shared branch. Goal: preserve or improve the high-recall lane around 1% coverage while improving aggregate regret relative to the fixed negative-tail branch.

title: Test feasible-first oracle policy on multiheavy
description: Add an alternate feasible-first oracle rollout mode that prefers the cheapest on-time path when one exists. Use it only on the training split for a matched plain-multiheavy scout while keeping validation and test manifests on the standard cost-first oracle policy.
close_reason: Scoped out without GPU run. Under the current benchmark contract, on-time feasibility is defined by the same cumulative _edge_cost already minimized by the oracle, so a train-only feasible-first oracle rollout is equivalent to the existing cost-first oracle except for tie cases.

title: Budget-conditioned evidence agreement experiment
description: Test whether conditioning the live evidence-agreement prototype gate on the intended defer budget improves coverage-specific calibration without re-anchoring the score to memory or reopening dead outer-budget conditioning on the memory branch.

title: Negative-cleanup lift support agreement mixture experiment
description: Test whether the live support-weighted agreement-mixture branch improves if it keeps the sharp negative-tail cleanup branch as the default score and only allows a gated nonnegative lift from the high-recall fixed negative-tail branch when that branch is distinctly stronger. Goal: recover some of the fixed branch's sparse-positive recall around 1% coverage without repeating the weak-middle behavior from blend and max-union variants.

title: Run 2+ seed follow-up on multiheavy exploit curriculum
Add verifier-backed path-feasibility filter for reranker under deeper_packets OOD
description: If reranking is revisited, use explicit path-feasibility or verifier-backed pruning instead of another generic scalar gate. Target the remaining deeper_packets6 seed312 failure first.
close_reason: Implemented verifier-backed path-feasibility filtering for the reranker, matched multiheavy in-distribution on seed312, and improved all three seed312 OOD suites while removing the catastrophic deeper_packets6 failure from the gated reranker path.

title: Memory-evidence agreement blend experiment
description: Add a cross-family prototype head that anchors on the live prototype-memory score and blends toward the evidence-calibrated agreement-mixture score only when richer branch evidence supports the lift, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid, prototype_memory_agree_blend_hybrid, prototype_agree_mix_hybrid, and prototype_evidence_agree_hybrid, and close it unless it improves the micro-budget or matched-band frontier without broad regression.

title: Budget-conditioned memory blend experiment
description: Add a prototype head that anchors on the live memory score but learns separate micro-budget and matched-band lift scores, collapse them into one deployable defer score, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid, prototype_memory_agree_blend_hybrid, and prototype_agree_mix_hybrid, and close it unless it improves either the micro-budget or matched-band frontier without broad regression.

title: Positive-lift evidence agreement experiment
description: Add a one-sided evidence-lift agreement prototype head that starts from the coverage-efficient agreement-mixture gate and only widens the blend when the dual geometry shows stronger positive evidence or cleaner negative evidence than the shared branch, evaluate it held-out against prototype_agree_mix_hybrid and prototype_evidence_agree_hybrid, and close it unless it improves sparse-positive recovery or matched-band quality without broad regression.

title: Test critical-decision oversampling on multiheavy
description: Add train-only decision-level oversampling for plain multiheavy, biasing minibatches toward low-slack and higher-packet-count decisions while keeping validation and test manifests unchanged. Goal: see whether stronger hard-case replay moves held-out regret and deadline behavior when smaller loss-only tweaks stayed flat.
close_reason: Completed matched 3-seed critical-decision oversampling scout. Train-only sampling weights materially changed minibatch pressure toward low-slack and multi-packet decisions, but all held-out rollouts still matched the plain multiheavy baseline exactly.

title: Contrastive evidence agreement experiment
description: Add a contrastive evidence-agreement prototype head that gates the shared-vs-dual blend using explicit shared/dual evidence deltas rather than raw branch evidence, evaluate it held-out against prototype_agree_mix_hybrid and prototype_evidence_agree_hybrid, and close it unless it improves sparse-positive recovery or matched-band quality without broad regression.

title: validation
description: Keep round8 validation green before large runs and after merge-back.
close_reason: round8 completed or explicitly scoped out

title: Branchwise max negative-cleanup support agreement mixture experiment
description: Test whether the live support-weighted agreement-mixture branch improves if shared and dual branches each take a max-style union between fixed negative-tail cleanup and the newer branch-strength sharp negative-tail cleanup before the outer agreement mixture, instead of taking the max only after the final mixed score. Success means preserving more of the fixed branch's recall without giving up the sharp branch's low-coverage quality.

title: Test conditional deployment for verifier-filter reranker
description: Plain multiheavy remains the robust default, but the verifier-filter reranker now shows strong 3-seed OOD gains with one matched in-distribution regression on seed313. Test a bounded deployment rule or checkpoint-selection contract that keeps plain multiheavy behavior on easy/matched suites while enabling verifier-filter reranking only on high-risk traffic/depth regimes.
close_reason: Implemented and tested shared-config conditional deployment for verifier-filter reranking. The policy fell back to multiheavy on matched suites and worsened shared OOD mean regret from 6.45 to 6.85 with no deadline-miss gain, so the branch is not deployable.

title: Audit policy invariance across round5 negative exploit tweaks
description: Build a shared-manifest checkpoint comparison for plain multiheavy versus recent negative round5 variants, measure decision/policy agreement, and use it to decide whether to stop spending cycles on local objective tweaks.
close_reason: Dropped per user request; no audit work landed

title: round9 compute-state experiment families
description: Run outer-step headroom, offline branch teacher, halting/continuation, mailbox, and route-persistence compute/state batches with matched evaluation.

title: Cascade prototype defer experiment
description: Add a regime-switching cascade prototype defer head that uses the ultra-low-coverage prototype_hybrid geometry in the sharp-positive regime and the agreement-mixture geometry in the broader matched-band regime, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid and prototype_agree_mix_hybrid, and close it unless it improves the sparse-positive frontier without broad regression.

title: Dual-projection prototype defer experiment
description: Add an asymmetric prototype defer head with separate positive and negative query projections, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid, and close it unless it improves stable-positive recovery or the hard near-tie band without broad regression.

title: Supported residual-regime evidence experiment
description: Add explicit positive evidence support gates to the anchored residual-regime evidence family, evaluate whether support-limited specialist lifts reduce false positives without losing sparse-positive recovery, and update reports.

title: Sharpness evidence agreement experiment
description: Add a sharpness-aware evidence-agreement prototype head that augments the current evidence gate with shared/dual positive and negative prototype sharpness features, evaluate it held-out against prototype_agree_mix_hybrid and prototype_evidence_agree_hybrid, and close it unless it improves sparse-positive recovery or matched-band quality without broad regression.

title: Memory-calibrated evidence agreement experiment
description: Test whether adding memory-anchor score and memory prototype evidence as gate inputs to the evidence-agreement prototype head improves low-coverage calibration without forcing a memory-anchored final score.

title: Round2 explore track: history-read and X1 rescue
description: Reproduce the current best explore baseline, implement B1 and B2, and run scouting/candidate sweeps with diagnostics.

title: Top2-negative-tail support agreement mixture experiment
description: Test whether the live support-weighted agreement-mixture branch improves if negative-tail cleanup preserves the top-2 negative matches and only suppresses the broader negative tail beneath them. Goal: keep the useful competing negative evidence while removing diffuse tail clutter, to improve the sharp-negative branch's aggregate quality without giving back the fixed negative-tail branch's sparse-positive recall.

title: Prototype adapter defer experiment
description: Add a prototype-plus-local-adapter defer head in the encoded prototype space, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid, and close it unless it improves stable-positive recovery without regressing the hard near-tie band.
notes: Testing a prototype-memory head with a tiny learned adapter over encoded prototype-space features, with and without the existing risk branch; compare held-out directly to prototype_hybrid and close unless Tier-1 recovery improves cleanly.

title: Round3 E3 reproducibility audit
description: Diagnose replay drift between archived and replayed E3 runs and write e3_repro_audit_round3.md.

title: Round7 frozen representation probe audit
close_reason: completed: frozen multiheavy probe audit landed with round7 probe tables

title: Risk-veto regime memory blend experiment
description: Add a prototype head that keeps the live memory-anchor geometry but applies explicit headroom and residual risk priors only as veto masks on regime-specialist lift gates, evaluate it held-out against prototype_memory_agree_blend_hybrid and the matched-band prototype leads, and close it unless it improves the micro-budget or matched-band frontier without broad regression.

title: Branch-strength sharp negative-tail support agreement mixture experiment
description: Test whether the live sharp negative-tail support-weighted agreement-mixture branch improves if the shared and dual negative banks use separate learned cleanup amplitudes instead of one global tail-strength ceiling. Success means preserving the sub-1% 75%/90.73% lane while improving recall around 1% or aggregate matched-band regret.

title: Test feasible-first hard target loss on multiheavy
description: Add a bounded feasible-first hard-target selection loss on top of plain multiheavy. When at least one valid on-time candidate exists, supervise the lowest-cost on-time candidate instead of only the default oracle next hop. Compare against the current plain multiheavy baseline on matched seeds and fixed evaluation manifests.
close_reason: Implemented feasible-first hard-target supervision using existing candidate on-time and cost labels and ran a matched 3-seed plain-multiheavy scout. The new target loss populated cleanly and changed selected epochs to 3/5/2, but the held-out rollout slightly worsened overall versus plain multiheavy, so feasible-first hard targets alone are not a useful next lever.

title: Round7 specialist-teacher audit
close_reason: completed: specialist-teacher family audited and closed; only heavy teacher moved and it degraded hard-slice target match

title: Regime-split memory blend experiment
description: Add a prototype head that keeps the live memory-anchor geometry but splits the one-sided outer lift into two learned regime specialists (high-headroom vs residual baseline-error style) and evaluate it held-out against prototype_memory_agree_blend_hybrid and the matched-band prototype leads; close it unless it improves the micro-budget or matched-band frontier without broad regression.

title: reporting
description: Update round8 reports, plots, portfolio ledger, and experiment summary.

title: Prototype suppressor defer experiment
description: Add a prototype defer head that preserves the live positive-vs-negative score but adds a dedicated harmful-memory suppressor bank, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid, and close it unless it improves stable-positive recovery or the hard near-tie band without broad regression.
notes: Testing whether the missing gain is mostly false-positive suppression from explicit harmful memories rather than richer positive routing. Variants: plain suppressor and suppressor+risk hybrid.

title: Evaluate multiheavy outer-step selection at inference
description: Test whether selecting among existing outer refinement steps at inference changes the held-out multiheavy policy materially, using the current E3/multiheavy checkpoint family and existing verifier/risk signals rather than new train-only loss tweaks.
close_reason: Completed outer-step selection scout; earliest_final_agreement matched final and non-final strategies were negative

title: Agreement-gated prototype mixture experiment
description: Add an agreement-gated mixture prototype defer head that anchors on the shared-projection score and only mixes in dual-projection geometry when the branches agree enough to trust it, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid and prototype_mixture_hybrid, and close it unless it improves the sparse-positive frontier without broad regression.

title: Audit corrected-split deadline slack and feasibility targets
Negative-cleanup blend support agreement mixture experiment
description: Test whether the live support-weighted agreement-mixture branch improves if it learns a per-state blend between the high-recall fixed negative-tail cleanup path and the higher-quality sharp negative-tail cleanup path. Goal: preserve more of the negative-tail branch's held-out recall around 1% coverage without giving back the sharp-negative branch's stronger aggregate regret below 1%.

title: Prototype gated-hybrid defer experiment
description: Add a risk-conditioned gating variant for the live prototype-memory defer head, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid, and close it unless it improves Tier-1 stable-positive recovery without regressing the hard near-tie band.
notes: Testing a risk-conditioned gated prototype head that modulates prototype scores instead of only adding a risk offset; compare held-out directly to prototype_hybrid and close unless Tier-1 recovery improves cleanly.

title: Round3 reporting and plots
description: Update portfolio balance, next actions, plots, and write continuation_report_round3.md.

title: Test slack-critical decision weighting on multiheavy
description: Add bounded slack-critical weighting to the main next-hop CE on plain multiheavy using existing candidate slack labels. Upweight decisions whose best available slack is small or negative, then compare against the current plain multiheavy baseline on matched seeds and fixed evaluation manifests.
close_reason: Implemented slack-critical weighting on the main next-hop loss using existing candidate slack labels and ran a matched 3-seed plain-multiheavy scout. The branch changed selected epochs to 4/2/2 and showed poor early checkpoints on two seeds, but all held-out rollout metrics matched the existing baseline exactly on all three seeds, so slack-critical weighting alone is not a useful next lever.

title: Soft-tail support agreement mixture experiment
description: Test whether the live support-weighted agreement-mixture branch improves if low-ranked prototype logits are softly suppressed relative to the per-bank winner instead of being hard-truncated by top-k pooling.

title: Anchored residual-regime evidence experiment
description: Keep the live evidence-agreement score as the anchor, add bounded headroom and baseline-error specialist lifts, evaluate held-out sparse-positive recovery, and update reports.

title: Selective-bank evidence agreement experiment
description: Add a per-state prototype-selection architecture inside the evidence-agreement family, evaluate held-out sparse-positive recovery, and update reports.

title: Test deadline-aware soft action targets on multiheavy
description: Add a bounded soft-target action loss on top of plain multiheavy using existing candidate_cost_to_go and candidate_on_time labels. Goal: change the learned policy directly without adding a new architecture. Compare against the current plain multiheavy baseline on matched seeds and fixed evaluation manifests.
close_reason: Implemented deadline-aware soft action targets using existing candidate cost/on-time labels and ran a matched 3-seed plain-multiheavy scout. The new loss populated cleanly and changed selected epochs, but held-out rollout metrics matched the existing baseline exactly on all three seeds, so soft target coupling alone is not a useful next lever.

title: Memory dual-lift blend experiment
description: Add a prototype head that anchors on the live memory score, computes both a score-only agreement lift and an evidence-aware agreement lift, takes the stronger positive lift per state, evaluates it held-out against prototype_memory_agree_blend_hybrid and prototype_evidence_agree_hybrid, and close it unless it improves the micro-budget or matched-band frontier without broad regression.

title: Teacher-signal evidence agreement experiment
description: Test whether adding auxiliary teacher-signal prediction heads to the live evidence-agreement prototype family improves sparse-positive calibration without hard-anchoring the output to memory or direct teacher-gain regression.

description: Build matched-seed E3 and X6 contender runs, launch paired jobs, and analyze the decision.

Goal: preserve or improve the 100% held-out sparse-positive recall around 1% coverage while improving sub-1% or matched-band quality.

Success means preserving the sharp branch below 1% coverage while recovering more of the fixed branch's 100% held-out sparse-positive recall lane around 1%.

Requires split-specific benchmark overrides so train can change while val/test stay fixed.
close_reason: Implemented split-specific benchmark overrides so train deadlines can tighten while validation/test manifests stay fixed. Ran a matched 3-seed plain-multiheavy scout with tighter training-only oracle-calibrated deadlines; held-out rollout metrics matched the existing baseline exactly on all three seeds, so tighter train deadlines alone are not a useful next lever.

title: Prototype specialist-bank defer experiment
description: Add a two-specialist prototype defer head that separates high-headroom and baseline-error positive memories, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid, and close it unless it improves stable-positive recovery without regressing the hard near-tie band.
notes: Testing a two-specialist prototype head with separate high-headroom and baseline-error positive banks plus shared negatives; compare held-out directly to prototype_hybrid and close unless Tier-1 recovery improves cleanly.

title: Branch-strength negative-cleanup max support agreement mixture experiment
description: Test whether the live support-weighted agreement-mixture branch improves if the newer branch-strength sharp negative-tail cleanup is combined with the older fixed negative-tail branch via a max-style nonnegative union. Success means preserving the branch-strength matched-band quality while recovering more of the fixed branch's 100% held-out sparse-positive recall lane around 1% coverage.

title: Teacher-margin memory blend experiment
description: Add a prototype head that anchors on the live memory-agreement blend but learns a small auxiliary correction-strength head from teacher gain signals, evaluate it held-out against prototype_memory_agree_blend_hybrid and the matched-band prototype leads, and close it unless it improves the micro-budget or matched-band frontier without broad regression.

title: Anchor-biased evidence agreement experiment
description: Add an anchor-biased evidence-agreement prototype head that keeps the richer shared/dual evidence-aware gate but adds an explicit conservative anchor toward the current agreement-mixture regime, evaluate it held-out against prototype_agree_mix_hybrid and prototype_evidence_agree_hybrid, and close it unless it improves sparse-positive coverage-efficiency without broad regression.

title: Run seed313 path-reranker contender on rebalanced suites
description: Extend the promising round-four path-reranker scout to a third matched seed on the rebalanced oracle_calibrated suites, then compare against the seed313 E3 and multiheavy baselines on regret, p95 regret, and deadline miss rate.
close_reason: Killed early after seed313 plateaued at 2.47 regret, 9.20 p95, and 68.8% deadline miss; standalone reranker not promoted.

title: Candidate-aware prototype defer experiment
notes: Implement a candidate-aware prototype defer head using top-2 pair features on the round12 cached split; compare held-out performance against prototype_hybrid and the round11 margin_regime reference.

title: Memory-agreement blend experiment
description: Add a cross-family prototype head that anchors on the live prototype-memory score and blends toward the agreement-mixture score only when the two branches agree enough to trust the broader correction, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid and prototype_agree_mix_hybrid, and close it unless it improves sparse-positive recovery or matched-band quality without broad regression.

title: Prototype interaction defer experiment
description: Add a score-aware prototype defer head that preserves the live prototype score but gives the residual branch a small set of prototype-score summary stats, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid, and close it unless it improves stable-positive recovery or the hard near-tie band without broad regression.
notes: Testing whether the live prototype lead is bottlenecked by a residual branch that cannot see the current prototype evidence state. Variants: additive interaction residual and gated interaction residual.

title: Round3 repo audit and validation
description: Audit current round-two state, write continuation_audit_round3.md, and rerun the fast green stack.

title: Shared-sharp negative-tail support agreement experiment
description: Test whether the live negative-tail and sharp-negative-tail gains can be combined by applying sharpness-gated negative-bank cleanup only on the shared branch while keeping fixed negative-tail cleanup on the dual branch. Goal: preserve the high-recall lane around 1% coverage while improving aggregate regret relative to the fixed negative-tail branch.

title: Learned-gate negative-tail support agreement mixture experiment
description: Test whether the live support-weighted agreement-mixture branch improves if shared and dual negative-tail cleanup gates are learned from internal negative-bank summary features such as top-gap and tail-mass gap, instead of using fixed hand-written sharpness or mass formulas. Success means preserving or improving the sub-1% 75%/90.73% lane or recovering more recall around 1% without giving back aggregate regret.

title: Prototype band-gated defer experiment
description: Add a score-band prototype defer head that preserves the live prototype score but only lets a residual branch act near the ambiguous prototype-score band, evaluate it held-out against prototype_hybrid, and close it unless it improves stable-positive recovery or the hard near-tie band without broad regression.
notes: Testing whether the live prototype lead is bottlenecked by residual spill outside the ambiguous score region. Variants: bandpass residual and bandpass residual with sharper gate.

title: Test deadline-aware pairwise ranking loss on multiheavy
description: Add a bounded pairwise ranking loss on top of plain multiheavy using existing candidate cost-to-go, on-time, and slack labels. Goal: change the learned policy more directly than soft targets by penalizing score orderings that place infeasible or higher-cost candidates above feasible lower-cost ones. Compare against the current plain multiheavy baseline on matched seeds and fixed evaluation manifests.
close_reason: Implemented deadline-aware pairwise action-ranking loss using existing candidate cost/on-time/slack labels and ran a matched 3-seed plain-multiheavy scout. The ranking loss populated cleanly and changed selected epochs to 2/2/3, but held-out rollout metrics matched the existing baseline exactly on all three seeds, so pairwise ranking alone is not a useful next lever.

title: Test DAgger-style state-refresh training on multiheavy
description: Add a bounded train-only state-refresh pass for plain multiheavy: run the current policy on train episodes, relabel visited states with oracle next-hop targets, and mix those decision records back into training while keeping validation and test manifests unchanged. Goal: test whether changing the supervised state distribution can move held-out regret and deadline behavior after loss-only and sampler-only variants stayed flat.

title: Prototype-memory ultra-low defer experiment
notes: Implement a learnable prototype-memory defer head on cached round12 teacher-bank data; evaluate against the stable-positive pack and margin_regime reference under ultra-low coverage budgets.

title: Regime-split evidence agreement experiment
description: Split the evidence-agreement prototype family into headroom and baseline-error regime banks, evaluate held-out sparse-positive recovery, and update reports.

title: Round6 repair branch
notes: Scoped out in round six because neither regime experts nor the plannerized decoder showed hard-case policy movement, so the repair prerequisite was not met.

title: Test risk-weighted checkpoint selection on multiheavy
description: Implement configurable checkpoint-selection weights in the trainer, then run a matched 3-seed plain-multiheavy scout that emphasizes p95 regret and deadline miss over solved-rate / local decision accuracy. Goal: see whether selection policy alone can improve held-out tail regret and miss rate without adding a new routing mechanism.
close_reason: Implemented configurable checkpoint-selection weights and ran a matched 3-seed plain-multiheavy scout. The risk-biased selector chose earlier checkpoints (epochs 3/2/2) but produced identical held-out rollout metrics to the existing multiheavy baseline on all three seeds, so selection-policy changes alone are not a useful next lever.

title: Round7 reporting and plot refresh
close_reason: completed: round7 reports, plots, portfolio ledger, and summary artifacts refreshed

title: counterfactual dataset build
description: Cache top-k counterfactual action values for hard near-tie states.
close_reason: round8 completed or explicitly scoped out

title: repo audit
description: Round8 repo audit and execution plan for near-tie critic/search work.
close_reason: round8 completed or explicitly scoped out

title: Round6 validation green and guardrail baseline reproduction
Asymmetric tail support agreement mixture experiment
description: Test whether adding a smaller positive-bank soft-tail cleanup on top of the new negative-tail support agreement-mixture branch can preserve 100% held-out stable-positive recall around 1% coverage while improving the sub-1% frontier.

title: Floor-gated sharp negative-tail support agreement experiment
description: Test whether the live sharp-negative-tail support-weighted agreement-mixture branch improves if its sharpness gate has a nonzero floor instead of ranging from zero to one. Goal: recover some of the fixed negative-tail branch's held-out recall around 1% coverage without repeating the additive over-suppression from the floor-plus-sharp experiment.

title: Floor-plus-sharp negative-tail support agreement experiment
description: Test whether the live sharp-negative-tail support-weighted agreement-mixture branch improves if it keeps a fixed negative-tail cleanup floor and adds extra sharpness-gated cleanup only when the negative bank is diffuse. Goal: preserve the older negative-tail branch's high held-out recall around 1% coverage while keeping the newer sharp-negative-tail branch's stronger aggregate regret around 0.75-1.0% coverage.
